commit.message:
fix: rewrite Reddit source to use Atom feed with native-tls

Reddit blocks requests based on TLS fingerprinting (rustls returns 403).
Switch to native-tls for Reddit client and parse Atom XML feeds instead
of JSON API. Add gzip support and atom_syndication dependency.

AI mode: JSON suggestion selection, regex extraction, command logging (#270)

AI mode previously replaced the buffer directly with raw command output.
This adds structured JSON output support (`[{"command": "...",
"description": "..."}]`), an interactive selection UI, and better
visibility into what's running.

## New: `src/ai_command.rs`
- `AiSuggestion` struct (`command` + `description`)
- `AiOutputSelection` — selection state with `move_up/down`,
`set_selected_by_idx`, `selected_command`
- `parse_ai_output(raw)` — debug-logs raw output, uses a
`OnceLock<Regex>` to find the JSON array start, bracket-walks to extract
the balanced array (tolerating prose before/after), parses via
`serde_json`

## Updated `ContentMode`
- `AiMode` is now a struct variant with `receiver` + `command_display:
String`
- New `AiOutputSelection(AiOutputSelection)` variant entered after
successful parse

## Interaction
- Up/Down arrows and mouse hover/click navigate the selection list
- Enter accepts the selected command → replaces buffer
- Escape cancels → back to Normal

## UI / Logging
- "Running ai mode..." → `"Running: <full command with buffer arg>"`
- `log::info!` logs the full command before spawning the background
thread
- Selection renders each entry as a description line + command line with
`▐` indicator

## Dependencies added
- `serde_json = "1.0"`, `regex = "1"`
- `Tag::AiResult(usize)` added to `content_builder` for mouse
hit-testing

commit.message:
feat: add timezone-aware cron scheduling

Replace Job::new_async with Job::new_async_tz to support timezone-aware
cron expression parsing. Add chrono-tz dependency and a 'timezone' config
option in [general] section (defaults to UTC for backward compatibility).

Users can now write cron expressions in their local timezone instead of
manually converting to UTC.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(solid): correct SurfaceNormal at degenerate sphere/orb poles (#98)

* fix(solid): correct SurfaceNormal at degenerate sphere/orb poles

At the south and north poles of a G4Sphere/G4Orb, the OCCT BRep stores
degenerate vertices rather than interior face points.  TryFindClosestFace
was rejecting BRepExtrema_IsVertex solutions, so SurfaceNormal fell through
to FallbackNormal() = (0, 0, 1) instead of the true outward normal.

Two cooperating fixes in G4OCCTSolid.cc:

1. TryFindClosestFace: accept BRepExtrema_IsVertex solutions.  The
   subsequent GeomAPI_ProjectPointOnSurf step re-derives UV parameters
   independently, so the vertex location itself is not used for the normal
   evaluation.

2. TryGetOutwardNormal: extend the parametric-boundary nudge to non-periodic
   U and V boundaries (previously only periodic seams were nudged).  Sphere
   poles land at V = ±π/2 = vFirst/vLast; the tiny initial nudge places
   |dP/dU| = R·sin(δ) right at IntersectionTolerance(), which can leave
   BRepLProp_SLProps::IsNormalDefined() false.  A retry loop with
   exponentially growing nudges (10⁻³ … 10⁴ × VResolution) walks away
   from the degenerate point until the normal is well-defined.

Fixes test_geometry_validation/g4sphere-sphere-r15-v1 (south pole normal
mismatch: imported returned (0,0,1) instead of (0,0,-1)) and also resolves
the same latent issue in g4orb-orb-r11-v1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(deps): recompile requirements lockfiles to resolve pip-compile conflict (#1221)

* Initial plan

* fix(deps): recompile requirements lockfiles to resolve FastAPI/Starlette version conflicts

The lockfiles were stale - all.txt pinned fastapi==0.121.0 and starlette==0.49.1 while base.in
required fastapi==0.135.1 and starlette==0.52.1. This caused pip-compile to fail when trying to
compile base.txt with all.txt as a constraint.

Recompiled all lockfiles to align with the source .in files.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(deps): compile ml.txt with CPU-only PyTorch to satisfy lockfile contract

- Use --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu for CPU-only wheels
- Remove all GPU-linked packages (cuda-*, nvidia-*, triton)
- torch and torchvision now use +cpu variants (2.10.0+cpu, 0.25.0+cpu)

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add handler.ts for all 22 github-intelligent-* surface modules

Create surface-specific handler implementations for: action, branch,
code-review, codespace, commit, deployment, fork, label, mention,
milestone, notification, package, page, project, reaction, release,
repository, security, sponsor, star, team, and wiki.

Each handler exports the full contract: buildPrompt, postResponse,
getSessionKey, getConcurrencyKey, getReactionTarget, and ReactionTarget.
Prompts and keys are customized per surface. Surfaces without a natural
comment target (fork, package, page, release, repository, sponsor, star,
team, wiki) gracefully log responses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: re-announce zone/row shortcuts when pressed repeatedly

Zone and battlefield row shortcut keys (C, G, X, S, W, B, A, R and
Shift variants) silently dropped the announcement when pressed again
if nothing had changed, because AnnouncementService suppresses
duplicate Normal-priority messages.

Like the life-total fix (PR #26), these are explicit user re-reads
and should always speak. Use High priority in:
- ZoneNavigator.NavigateToZone (C, G, Shift+G, X, Shift+X, S, W)
- ZoneNavigator.AnnounceOpponentHandCount (Shift+C)
- BattlefieldNavigator.NavigateToRow (B, Shift+B, A, Shift+A, R, Shift+R)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(tools): exclude root AXIS2_PLACEMENT_3D from translate_step.py shift (#97)

* fix(tools): exclude root AXIS2_PLACEMENT_3D from translate_step.py shift

The root AXIS2_PLACEMENT_3D inside ADVANCED_BREP_SHAPE_REPRESENTATION
defines the BREP coordinate frame. OCCT applies it as a TopLoc_Location
when reading the file, so shifting its CARTESIAN_POINT caused a double
translation: internal geometry shifted by -dz AND the frame shifted by
-dz, resulting in net shift of -2*dz.

Fix translate_step.py to parse out the root AXIS2_PLACEMENT_3D for each
ADVANCED_BREP_SHAPE_REPRESENTATION and exclude its CARTESIAN_POINT from
the coordinate shift. Regenerate G4Tubs and G4Cons shape.step with the
corrected tool.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(fixtures): center G4Box, G4Tubs, G4Cons STEP shapes at origin (#96)

* fix(fixtures): center G4Box, G4Tubs, G4Cons STEP shapes at origin

DRAWEXE primitive commands (box, pcylinder, pcone) place one corner or
the bottom face at the OCCT origin, but the corresponding Geant4 solids
(G4Box, G4Tubs, G4Cons) are centered at the origin. This mismatch caused
~2038/2048 DistanceToOut mismatches for G4Box in the benchmark comparison.

Fix each fixture's generate.tcl to center the shape:
- G4Box:  box seed_box -10 -15 -20 20 30 40  (corner at half-extents)
- G4Tubs: translate seed_tubs 0 0 -17.5       (shift by -height/2)
- G4Cons: translate seed_cons 0 0 -12         (shift by -height/2)

Regenerate shape.step for all three fixtures and update draw_command in
provenance.yaml to match the new generation commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* fix(fixtures): center G4Tubs and G4Cons STEP shapes at origin

DRAWEXE's pcylinder and pcone commands place the bottom face at z=0,
but G4Tubs and G4Cons are centered at the origin. Fix generate.tcl
for both fixtures to post-process the STEP file via translate_step.py
(a new tool, analogous to normalize_step_header.py) which shifts all
3D CARTESIAN_POINTs by the required offset without touching the 2D
UV parametric points.

Also add src/tests/fixtures/geometry/tools/translate_step.py.

G4Tubs: shift (0, 0, -17.5) so cylinder spans z=-17.5..+17.5
G4Cons: shift (0, 0, -12) so cone spans z=-12..+12

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Initial scaffolding: SwiftUI MVVM app with full project structure

- SwiftUI app with 4 tabs: Weekly Releases, Collection, Recommendations, Search
- SwiftData models: Comic, Series, Creator, UserReview, ReadingStatus
- Service layer: ComicVineService, ClaudeService, WebSearchService, RecommendationEngine
- All services protocol-based for testability with mock implementations
- ViewModels: WeeklyReleases, ComicDetail, Collection, Recommendations, Search
- Views: Shared components (CoverImage, RatingBadge, StatusPicker)
- Utilities: APIKeyManager (Keychain), DateFormatters, ImageCache
- 16 unit tests (Swift Testing) + 5 UI tests (XCTest)
- Makefile with build/test/lint/setup targets
- Pre-push git hook (build + unit tests gate)
- XcodeGen project.yml for reproducible project generation
- Agent-ready: .github/copilot-instructions.md with architecture + test commands
- SwiftLint configuration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: ephemeral ECS debugging agent with S3 storage and UI redesign

- Remove PostgreSQL; replace with ephemeral local cache + S3 (LocalStack for dev)
- Consolidate agent/api/ui into single container (one ECS task)
- Agent: reads CloudWatch logs, calls Bedrock (Claude), records commands/reasoning
- API: stateless FastAPI serving from /data/cache + S3 fallback
- Local dev mode: LOCAL_DEV=true uses sample-data stubs, no AWS creds needed
- Docker Compose: LocalStack S3 with healthcheck, race-condition-free startup
- Security fixes: non-root appuser, .dockerignore, shell injection fix, Next.js upgrade
- UI redesign: icon KPI cards, AWS/Slack icons, Slack notifications pane, charts
